diagrams.title: A* Search Evaluation Function
diagrams.description: Shows how f(n) combines g(n) and h(n) to evaluate node promise
elements: Intermediate node N with g(N)=cost from S, h(N)=estimated cost to G
Nodes expanded in order of increasing f(n)
diagrams.purpose: Understand the core formula of informed search

diagrams.title: Admissible vs Consistent Heuristic
diagrams.description: Visual comparison showing admissibility (lower bound) and consistency (triangle inequality)
elements: Admissible: h(A) ≤ cost(A,goal) shown as underestimation
Consistent: h(A) ≤ cost(A,B)+h(B) shown as path property
Counter-example showing admissible but not consistent possible

diagrams.title: Greedy vs A* Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison of search paths taken by both algorithms
elements: Greedy path: straight to goal but suboptimal
A* path: explores more but finds optimal
Node expansion order shown with numbers
f(n), g(n), h(n) values at each node
diagrams.purpose: See why h(n) alone is insufficient for optimality
